name: Attio
description: Drive Attio (modern relationship-graph CRM) from any AI agent: records (people, companies, deals, custom objects), lists, tasks, notes. 12 tools, Bearer auth.
instructions:
This connector uses the Attio REST API v2 (developers.attio.com).

**Setup**:
1. Sign in to Attio → top-right avatar → **API & access → Create an access token** (or set up an OAuth app).
2. Pick scopes: at minimum `object_configuration:read`, `record_permission:read`, `record_permission:read-write`, `list_configuration:read`, `note:read-write`.
3. Copy the token. Set `ATTIO_ACCESS_TOKEN`.

**Authentication**: `Authorization: Bearer ${ATTIO_ACCESS_TOKEN}`.

**Object model**: Attio is highly schema-flexible. Default objects are `people`, `companies`, `deals`, `users`, `workspaces`. You can add custom objects (object_slug). Each object has attributes (fields) — different per workspace.

**Records have a path-style ID**: `{object: 'people', record_id: 'abc-123-uuid'}`. Use `attio_list_objects` to see what's available.

**Attributes / Values**: writing values requires the right shape per attribute type (text, number, date, status, select, multi-select, email-address, phone-number, currency, location, personal-name, record-reference). Wrap values in arrays: `{values: {email_addresses: [{email_address:'a@b.com'}]}}`.

**Lists**: views/collections of records. Different from CRMs that use 'segments' or 'tags'. Each list has entries (record references).

**Pagination**: `limit + offset`. Default 100, max 1000.

**Rate limits**: 100 req per 10 sec per token. On 429 back off.

**Out of scope here**: object/attribute schema CRUD, workspaces management, webhook subscription management.

tools.name: attio_self
tools.description: Return the token's identity (workspace_id, workspace_name, access_token id, permissions).

tools.name: attio_list_objects
tools.description: List all objects defined in the workspace (built-in + custom). Returns id, api_slug, singular_noun, plural_noun, created_at.

tools.name: attio_list_object_attributes
tools.description: List attributes (fields) on an object. Returns id, api_slug, title, type (text/number/date/status/select/email-address/...), is_required, is_unique.

tools.name: attio_query_records
tools.description: Query records on an object with filters + sorting.

tools.name: attio_get_record
tools.description: Fetch a single record by object + record_id.

tools.name: attio_create_record
tools.description: Create a record. Values are nested under `values` and keyed by attribute api_slug. Most attributes accept ARRAYS even for single-value (Attio's design for multi-value support).

data.description: {values: {api_slug: <value or [array of typed values]>, ...}}. E.g. for a person: {name:[{first_name:'Jane',last_name:'Doe'}], email_addresses:[{email_address:'a@b.com'}]}.

tools.name: attio_update_record
tools.description: Update a record (PATCH semantics — only updates passed values). Use append=true to add to multi-value, false to replace.

tools.name: attio_assert_record
tools.description: Upsert by a unique attribute (e.g. email). Required: matching_attribute parameter to identify the unique attribute. Creates if missing, updates if exists.

tools.name: attio_list_lists
tools.description: List the workspace's lists (views/collections of records). Returns id, name, parent_object, workspace_access.

tools.name: attio_add_record_to_list
tools.description: Add a record (as a list entry) to a list. Required: listId + parent_record_id (the source record).

parent_object.description: Filter to notes on a specific object slug.

parent_record_id.description: Filter to notes on a specific record.

tools.name: attio_create_note
tools.description: Add a note to a record. format='plaintext' (default) or 'markdown'.

data.description: {parent_object: 'people', parent_record_id: 'uuid', title?: 'Meeting notes', format:'plaintext'|'markdown', content:'Discussed Q3...'}.
